goal: Give enterprise security managers a self-serve platform to create, test, and publish custom Adversary Emulation Pathways — AI-powered social engineering chatbot personas — without Dune operator involvement, reducing AEP creation time from ~1 day to under 2 hours.
user_segments: Customer — Security Manager (primary: CISO, security manager, or Red Teaming owner; fills form, tests, refines, publishes)
Customer — Reviewer (secondary: optional sign-off role configured per account; read-only view + approve/reject)
Dune Operator (internal: full visibility, validation override, operator refinement, Operator Assist flag)
Dune Admin (platform-level: all Operator permissions + template management, global ban list)
success_metrics: AEP creation cycle time < 2 hours (baseline: ~1 day)
Dune operator touchpoints per AEP: 0 for standard cases (baseline: 2-3 required)
Structural validation pass rate on first publish > 95% (baseline: ~60%)
Avg refinement rounds before customer approves < 3
AEP reuse rate (cloned from library vs. net-new) > 30%
constraints: 3-stage builder flow (Stage 1: Form + Examples, Stage 2: Live Chat Test, Stage 3: Prompt Refinement) — stages are revisitable non-linearly after initial generation
Stage 1 intake form has 6 structured sections: Account Context, Attack Scenario, Systems at Risk, Rules & Compliance, Cultural Context, Termination Logic
Example upload: 1-5 files per AEP; accepted formats: .txt, .docx, .png, .jpg (OCR); examples purged after generation, metadata retained
Channel multi-select: WhatsApp, Telegram, Viber, Facebook Messenger, SMS, Instagram, Microsoft Teams, Signal
Generation: P95 < 45s without examples, P95 < 90s with image OCR; labeled progress stages required; partial retry on section failure
Stage 2 uses full production persona runtime — identical to live campaign behavior
Stage 2: state label visible to customer during test; never visible to employees in live campaign
Stage 2: 4 archetype starter responses (Curious, Skeptical, Hostile, Compliant) as one-click injection; unlimited test sessions
Stage 3: targeted field-level edits only (not full regeneration); diff view with per-field accept/reject; cannot remove global guardrails
Published AEPs are immutable — changes require cloning to new version (REQ-SS-12)
Validation layer: blocking checks + warnings requiring explicit acknowledgment before publish
AEP Library: table view with search (< 500ms), filter by status/channel/compliance/date/campaign, clone, archive, version history
Template system: Dune-curated templates (6 at launch); any customer AEP can be promoted to template by Dune Admin
Dune Operator view: same UI as customer with permission overlay; can view raw JSON, override warnings, submit refinement on behalf
Campaign integration: Active AEP required to create/schedule/launch a campaign
Refinement prompts that remove global guardrails (hate speech, violence, PII collection) are blocked (REQ-SS-11)
Example data used for generation LLM call only; purged after; metadata retained
RBAC: Security Manager creates/edits/publishes; Reviewer approves only; Operator full access; Admin platform-level
assumptions: Workflow Steps, Branching Logic, and all AEP content are AI-generated from the Stage 1 form + examples — customers do not manually author JSON or raw prompts
Admin plays the employee (target) role manually in Stage 2 simulator
6 fixed conversation states in the state machine (1_INITIAL through 6_VULNERABILITY) — customers configure scripts, not states
Published AEPs are locked to the version at campaign creation; hot-swapping mid-campaign is not supported in Phase 1
AEP library scope is per customer organization (one library per tenant) — per-program scoping is Phase 2+ pending PRD clarification
Hard limit on Active AEPs per tenant is not confirmed in PRD — archiving is the management mechanism
UI is English only; persona content supports all UTF-8 character sets (Tagalog, Hinglish, Taglish, Arabic, etc.)
gaps: Whether a Reviewer is ever mandatory (blocking gate) or always optional — affects publish flow
Multi-channel deployment model: simultaneous vs. one-channel-per-campaign
AEP library scope: per-organization vs. per-account/program
Hard limit on Active AEPs per tenant — not specified in PRD
Instigation threshold enum may be too coarse for real account variation
Refinement prompt cross-field cascading behavior — affects diff view design
Reviewer notification surface and approval UI not defined in PRD
Auto-save behavior for Stage 1 form mid-completion not specified
edge_cases: Generation partial failure: some sections succeed, others fail — per-section retry with preserved form inputs
OCR failure on image upload: must surface per-file error with option to paste text instead
Example upload exceeds 5 files: reject with clear max-limit message
Example file format unsupported: clear format error with accepted format list
Refinement prompt attempts to remove guardrails: blocked with plain-language explanation
Publish with warnings: each warning requires individual checkbox acknowledgment
Archive blocked by active campaign: error names blocking campaigns
Clone from active/archived AEP: creates new Draft at incremented version with lineage metadata
No Active AEPs when customer attempts to create campaign: prompt links to AEP Builder
Template picker unavailable: error state with retry CTA
Empty library (first visit): empty state with New AEP + Start from Template CTAs
Reviewer configured: AEP enters Pending Review after publish; campaign in draft but cannot launch until approved
Operator Assist: customer receives notification with operator diff summary; can review before next publish
Stage 2 persona response timeout: per-message timeout handling with retry or session reset option
Generation timeout: retry option with partial section recovery
Draft AEP with 0 test sessions: Publish button disabled with tooltip
Draft AEP with 1 test session: Publish allowed with warning requiring acknowledgment
AEP attached to completed campaign: Archive allowed with confirmation; historical record preserved
Instigation threshold set to None: warning on publish — persona will push on all resistance

critical_questions.question: Is there a scenario where a Dune operator must approve an AEP before it goes live? Or is the optional Reviewer role + validation layer sufficient for all standard cases?
Does one AEP support simultaneous deployment across multiple channels in one campaign, or is it one channel per campaign?
Is the AEP library scoped per customer organization or per account/program?
Does Stage 2 use the full production LLM or a lighter model? What is the cost-per-session assumption?
Can a single refinement prompt cascade changes across dependent field groups (e.g., opening message update also updates 1_INITIAL state script)?
Is the instigation threshold enum (none/soft_stop/hard_stop) sufficient or does it need a richer model?
Is there a hard limit on Active AEPs per tenant, or is archiving the management mechanism?
What is the Reviewer notification surface and approval UI?
What is the auto-save behavior for Stage 1 form mid-completion?

design_risks: Generation wait time (45-90s) with static feedback will feel broken — labeled progress stages must visibly advance
Stage 3 diff view is highest-stakes screen — if hard to parse, customers rubber-stamp or reject all changes, negating the refinement model
Refinement loop without exit signal — customers may publish too early or never publish without a quality/completion indicator
Templates misrepresented as finished AEPs — customers may skip customization and deploy generic personas
Validation warnings becoming click-through if they fire too frequently — reserve for genuinely high-risk states only
Operator Assist trust asymmetry — operator modifications without prior consent require prominent notification with diff and rejection path
teaching_notes: AEP is not a single prompt — it includes system prompt, classifier prompt, 6-state state machine, detection arrays, cultural nuance packages, termination logic, and bot name lists. Builder abstracts this; designer must understand the model to make good disclosure decisions.
6 fixed conversation states (1_INITIAL through 6_VULNERABILITY) — customers configure scripts/transitions, not states themselves
Published AEPs are immutable by design (REQ-SS-12) — changes require cloning; version history is load-bearing UX
Example upload is optional but significantly improves generation quality per PRD Appendix
Template lineage tracked in metadata.templateLineage — surface in library for reuse metric
Game changers = employees in Dune internal language — customer-facing UI should use employee
Phase 1 target Q3 2026; Phase 2 (cross-AEP analytics, multi-version comparison, webhook reviewer) Q4 2026
